taxonomy: Ataccia integrifolia is classified as a plant species within the family Fabaceae. This classification suggests its relationship to other legumes, which are known for their distinctive seed pods and nitrogen-fixing abilities. Further classification places Ataccia integrifolia within the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies of the Fabaceae family. The genus Ataccia itself is part of the tribe Phaseoleae, which includes other vining legumes. Overall, Ataccia integrifolia's classification indicates its close affinity with other vining legumes and its potential for nitrogen fixation.
